documentation:
# clear-html ```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/clear-html.svg :alt: PyPI Version :target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/clear-html ``` ```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/clear-html.svg :alt: Supported Python Versions :target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/clear-html ``` ```{image} https://github.com/zytedata/clear-html/workflows/tox/badge.svg :alt: Build Status :target: https://github.com/zytedata/clear-html/actions ``` ```{image} https://codecov.io/github/zytedata/clear-html/coverage.svg?branch=master :alt: Coverage report :target: https://codecov.io/gh/zytedata/clear-html ``` Clean and normalize HTML. Preserve embeddings (e.g. Twitter, Instagram, etc) ```{contents} ``` ## Quick start ### Installation Install the library with pip: ``` pip install clear-html ``` ### Usage Example usage with lxml: ```python from lxml.html import fromstring from clear_html import clean_node, cleaned_node_to_html html="""
Some text to be
cleaned up!
""" node = fromstring(html) cleaned_node = clean_node(node) cleaned_html = cleaned_node_to_html(cleaned_node) print(cleaned_html) ``` Example usage with Parsel: ```python from parsel import Selector from clear_html import clean_node, cleaned_node_to_html selector = Selector(text="""

Hello!

Some text to be
cleaned up!
""") selector = selector.css("#main_content") cleaned_node = clean_node(selector[0].root) cleaned_html = cleaned_node_to_html(cleaned_node) print(cleaned_html) ``` Both of the different approaches above would print the following: ```HTML

Some text to be

cleaned up!

``` Other interesting functions: - `cleaned_node_to_text`: convert the cleaned node to plain text - `formatted_text.clean_doc`: low level method to control more aspects of the cleaning up

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.headings.min_heading
documentation: Return the min heading level in the document

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.headings.normalize_headings_level
documentation:
Normalizes headings in the doc so that the lowest level is always 2. If six levels document is found, the last level is replaced by `

` Nodes in the white list are ignored. ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("

") >>> normalize_headings_level(html) >>> tostring(html).decode() '

' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("

Holaque talcolega
") >>> normalize_headings_level(html) >>> tostring(html).decode() '

Holaque talcolega

' ```

classes.name: clear_html.formatted_text.cleaner.BodyCleaner
documentation:
Cleaner based on lxml Cleaner but with some modifications.

First is that the following options that ask for tag removal but
keeping content will be ignored: `page_structure`, `embedded`,
`forms`, `annoying_tags`. Only tags not killed by the rest of options
and that are not present in allow_tags will be dropped keeping content.

`nodes_whitelist`:

: A set of nodes to ignore in the cleaning up. They are both ignored
  in terms of element deletion or attribute cleaning.

`allow_data_attrs`:

: If true, attributes data-\* are not removed

`allow_tags`:

: Drop tags not in this set (keeping content). The cleaner try to do
  its best to respect text separation by inserting double br tags in
  some cases.

modules.name: clear_html.formatted_text
documentation:
Article body formatting module. Cleanup, normalize and format. Part of the cleanup process involves removing tags but keeping the content. There are two kinds of tags in HTML. The inline ones are used within the paragraphs (i.e. strong or em) and when removed but keeping the content no extra spacing should be included. For example: `

I would like to visit Spain this summer

` should be simplified in the clean up process to `

I would like to visit Spain this summer

` By the other side, block tags (i.e. div) are used to divide blocks of content. And their behaviour should be the same than paragraphs. For example, the cleaning process should convert `
This is first paragraph
And this second
` to `

This is first paragraph

And this second

` The body cleaner is doing that in two stages. The first one is separating these two sentences by double br: `This is first paragraph

And this second.` In a second stage the double or more consecutive BRs are converted into the final paragraphs (it is a good normalization practise even if they were already part of original page). The logic regarding when to introduce a double br is not always as simple as these shown in the examples before, but the general idea is that content in different blocks should be separated by at least one block tag or by double br if there is a need to remove the block tags and because of that the consecutive content would not be then separated by a block tag anymore. Main approach is to update the document inline, without doing copies. Steps: : - Look for particular embeddings, adapt html and freeze concerning nodes - Remove tags not accepted in our simplified html, but keeping spacing information. This is done by introducing double br in some cases. - Apply some rules to normalize html: headings, figures, clean incomplete structures. - Convert consecutive chunks of text into paragraphs (this remove the double br introduced in parts of the process). - Ensure that not block tags are inside inline tags (this is not allowed). - Finally html is formatted a little bit to have a good looking.

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.enclose_media_within_figure
documentation:
Ensures all media (images, videos, etc) are enclosed within figures.
If possible, images with
a link also includes the link within the figure element.

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.top_level_media_within_figure
documentation:
Enclose top level isolated multimedia into figures. In other words, paragraphs containing only a single media element are replaced by a figure. Nodes in the white list are ignored. ```pycon >>> apply = _test_fn(top_level_media_within_figure) ``` ```pycon >>> apply("

") '
' >>> apply("

t

") '

t

' >>> apply("

a

") '

a

' >>> apply("

a

") '

a

' >>> apply("

a

") '

a

' >>> apply("

") '
' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.infer_img_url_from_data_src_attr
documentation:
Fills src attribute from data-src for img tags. It is common to see img tags without src attribute but with data-src >>> html = fromstring("
") >>> infer_img_url_from_data_src_attr(html) >>> tostring(html).decode() '
'

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.create_figures_from_isolated_figcaptions
documentation:
Wraps isolated figcaptions with the content above and form a new figure. Mutates node. ```pycon >>> html = fromstring( ... "
" ... "" ... "" ... "" ... "

text3

" ... "
" ... "" ... "" ... "" ... "
") >>> create_figures_from_isolated_figcaptions(html) >>> tostring(html).decode() '

text3


' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring( ... "
" ... "" ... "" ... "" ... "
" ... "
") >>> create_figures_from_isolated_figcaptions(html) >>> clean_incomplete_structures(html, MUST_ANCESTORS_FOR_KEEP_CONTENT) >>> tostring(html).decode() '
' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.fuse_figcaptions
documentation: Fuses first block of consecutive figcaptions and remove the rest found. ```pycon >>> fuse = _test_fn(fuse_figcaptions) ``` ```pycon >>> fuse("") '' ``` ```pycon >>> fuse("") '' ``` ```pycon >>> fuse("") '' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.clean_figcaptions_html
documentation:
Simplifies figcapion html \>>> html = fromstring("\
\\
") \>>> clean_figcaptions_html(html) \>>> tostring(html).decode() '\
\\
'

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.remove_figures_without_content
documentation:
Removes figures that has no content apart of the figure caption. This can happen for some pages that inject the content with JS ```pycon >>> remove = _test_fn(remove_figures_without_content) ``` ```pycon >>> remove("
") '
' ``` ```pycon >>> remove("
heyJohn
") '
hey

John
' ``` ```pycon >>> remove("
") '
' ``` ```pycon >>> remove("
hey
") '
hey
' ``` ```pycon >>> remove("
") '
' ``` ```pycon >>> remove("
") '
' ``` ```pycon >>> remove("") '' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.figures.clean_double_br_above_figcaption
documentation: Some weird cases like when figure is implemented with tables we can end having a double br before figcaptions. For example in this case `` the clean_incomplete_structures will introduce the double br when cleaning the table elements. I have not a general solution for that so by now this function is used to mitigate this effect. ```pycon >>> clean = _test_fn(clean_double_br_above_figcaption) >>> clean("") '' ``` ```pycon >>> clean("") '' ``` ```pycon >>> clean("") '' ``` ```pycon >>> clean("") '' ``` ```pycon >>> clean("") '' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.main.clean_doc
documentation:
Clean the tree. Mutate the doc. Root node could change,
so final root is returned to avoid this effect. Nodes in `node_whitelist`
are preserved intact.

See also a description of the overall approach in
clear_html/formatted_text/\_\_init\_\_.py

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.main.paragraphy
documentation:
Ensures all textual content is inside a paragraph for first level.
Removes sequences of consecutive br tags enclosing surroundings into
paragraphs. Note that these kind of double
br sequences could have been introduced by `BodyCleaner` function,
of calls to `drop_tag_preserve_spacing`
and is in this function where we convert them to paragraphs
when possible. Document is updated inline.

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.main.almost_pretty_format
documentation:
Format doc to have a good looking when serialized as html. Only modifying first level of the body which is safe (formatting inner elements is not that safe). One line of separation for first level elements and some leading and trailing striping for better looking >>> from lxml.html import fromstring >>> html = "

1

2

3rd 

 pre
" >>> html = fromstring(html) >>> almost_pretty_format(html) >>> print(tostring(html).decode())

1

2

3rd

 pre

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.main.make_links_absolute
documentation:
Like doc.make_links_absolute which ignores errors,
but also does not fail on urls with escape chars, skipping them instead.

modules.name: clear_html.html_embeddings
documentation:
The goal of this module is to detect the embeddings from different
providers and modify the
HTML accordingly so that they can be integrated in the resultant
HTML without change.

functions.name: clear_html.html_embeddings.integrate_embeddings
documentation:
Integrate all embeddings found in the provided document.
Return a set of nodes that should be preserved 'as is' in the
clean up process

functions.name: clear_html.html_embeddings._include_also_children
documentation:
Include all children to the whitelist with an optional `preprocessor`
Callable that is applied to all elements in the `to_whitelist` input.

functions.name: clear_html.html_embeddings._include_subtree
documentation: Includes all nodes in subtrees of nodes in the set

functions.name: clear_html.html_embeddings._nodes_for_classes
documentation: Return a set with nodes having any of the classes in the list

classes.name: clear_html.body_annotations.BodyAnnotations
documentation:
Dict like structure that saves and reads from disk and that
stores BodyAnnotation values for each item id.

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.translate_tags
documentation:
Translate tag names (i.e. b -> strong). Mutates the doc. Nodes in the white list are ignored. >>> html = fromstring("

text

") >>> translate_tags(html) >>> tostring(html).decode() '

text

'

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.set_article_tag_as_root
documentation:
Ensures that root tag is `article`. It can return a new element. Mutates the doc. > ```pycon > >>> html = fromstring("

text

") > >>> tostring(set_article_tag_as_root(html)).decode() > '

text

' > ``` > > ```pycon > >>> html = fromstring("

text

") > >>> tostring(set_article_tag_as_root(html)).decode() > '

text

' > ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.wrap_tags
documentation: Enclose the elements with tag `to_be_enclosed_tags` within a tag `enclosing_tag` if they are not already enclosed, that is, if `enclosing_tag` is not already an ancestor. All transparent tags without more content than the element to enclose itself will be also included in the enclosed element (useful for enclosing `` into `

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.remove_empty_tags
documentation:
Removes empty tags, but skipping the `white_list` ones ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("

") >>> remove_empty_tags(html) >>> tostring(html).decode() '
' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("

") >>> remove_empty_tags(html, {'p'}) >>> tostring(html).decode() '

' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.drop_tag_preserve_spacing
documentation:
Drops a tag keeping its content. If element to be removed
is a block element, leading or trailing double br tags would
be introduced to preserve spacing. If preserve_content is
false, the entire tree will be deleted (but preserving spacing).

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.double_br
documentation: True if doc and next element are "br" tags without text in between.

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.has_no_content
documentation:
Checks if a node contains content. A node has content if it has any text in the tree or if has any tags that handle non textual content like `img` or `iframe`. `br`, `dt`, `dd`, `td` tags are considered as non content. Implementation detail: a copy of the doc is done. ```pycon >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
hello
")) False >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
")) True >>> has_no_content(fromstring("")) False >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
")) False >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
")) True >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
")) True >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
hey
")) False >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
hey
")) False >>> has_no_content(fromstring("
hey
")) False ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.is_empty
documentation:
Checks if given doc is an empty tag or tag formed with empty tags. `tags_with_content_even_if_empty` tags are considered as having content even if empty. ```pycon >>> is_empty(fromstring("
hello
")) False >>> is_empty(fromstring("
")) True >>> is_empty(fromstring("
")) True >>> is_empty(fromstring("
")) True >>> is_empty(fromstring("
a
")) False >>> is_empty(fromstring("
a
")) False >>> is_empty(fromstring("
a
")) False >>> is_empty(fromstring("
a
a
")) False ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.is_phrasing_content
documentation:
'Phrasing content is the text of the document, as well as elements that
mark up that text at the intra-paragraph level'
(see https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#phrasing-content). This method return
true if the element tag is one of those mark up allowed in paragraphs.
Unknown tags are considered as phrasing elements by default.

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.group_with_previous_content_block
documentation:
Return a ChildrenSlice that groups current node content block with previous content block. Return None if doc is the root. ```pycon >>> tostr = lambda x: f"{x.node.tag}, {x.start}, {x.end}" if x else 'None' >>> html = fromstring("

hey

") >>> tostr(group_with_previous_content_block(html.find(".//figcaption"))) 'article, 0, 3' ``` ```pycon >>> tostr = lambda x: f"{x.node.tag}, {x.start}, {x.end}" if x else 'None' >>> html = fromstring("

hey

end
") >>> tostr(group_with_previous_content_block(html.find(".//figcaption"))) 'div, 0, 2' ``` ```pycon >>> tostr = lambda x: f"{x.node.tag}, {x.start}, {x.end}" if x else 'None' >>> html = fromstring("

hey

end
") >>> tostr(group_with_previous_content_block(html.find(".//figcaption"))) 'None' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring( ... "

hey

text

") >>> tostr(group_with_previous_content_block(html.find(".//figcaption"))) 'None' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring( ... "

hey

text

") >>> tostr(group_with_previous_content_block(html.find(".//figcaption"))) 'None' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring( ... "

hey


") >>> tostr(group_with_previous_content_block(html.find(".//figcaption"))) 'article, 0, 4' ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.find_previous_non_empty_sibling
documentation:
```pycon >>> html = fromstring("
end
t
") >>> find_previous_non_empty_sibling(html.find(".//figcaption")) ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("
end
") >>> find_previous_non_empty_sibling(html.find(".//figcaption")) 0 ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("
") >>> find_previous_non_empty_sibling(html.find(".//figcaption")) ```

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.clean_incomplete_structures
documentation:
Drop tags (keeping content) of incomplete structures. For example, removes a td element if not belonging to any table. Never clean the base element. If preserve_content is false then nodes are completely removed without keeping content (but preserving spacing). Nodes in the white list are ignored. >>> def clean(html, rules=MUST_ANCESTORS_FOR_KEEP_CONTENT, ... preserve_content=True): ... html = fromstring(html) ... clean_incomplete_structures(html, rules, preserve_content) ... print(tostring(html).decode()) >>> clean("
pre
text
post
")
pre
text
post
>>> clean("
key
value
")
key

value
>>> clean("
A
key
text
value
to preserve", preserve_content=False)
A

text

to preserve
>>> clean("
pre
key
value
post")
pre

key

value

post
>>> clean("
prepost", MUST_ANCESTORS_FOR_DROP_CONTENT, False)
pre

post

functions.name: clear_html.formatted_text.utils.kill_tag_content
documentation:
Removes the content of all these tags found in the doc ```pycon >>> def kill(html): ... html = fromstring(html) ... kill_tag_content(html, 'iframe') ... print(tostring(html).decode()) ``` ```pycon >>> kill('
')
``` ```pycon >>> kill('
')
``` ```pycon >>> kill('
a
aa
a
')
a
aa
a
```

functions.name: clear_html.clean.cleaned_node_to_text
documentation:
Format the given html tree as plain text, applying particular exclusions
only applied to plain text (i.e. remove figure captions).
Provided node should have been already cleaned to have expected output

If provided, the optional `text_extractor` will be used to extract text
from the given input node. Otherwise, `html_text.extract_text` is used.

functions.name: clear_html.clean.cleaned_node_to_html
documentation:
Format the given html tree as html string. Provided node should have been already cleaned to have expected output ```pycon >>> from lxml.html import fromstring >>> html = '
' >>> html = cleaned_node_to_html(fromstring(html)) >>> print(html)
```

functions.name: clear_html.clean.clean_node
documentation:
Normalize the given lxml node. The resultant node contains cleaned HTML, with embeddings preserved. Returns a copy so that the original node remains untouched. ```pycon >>> from lxml.html import fromstring >>> html = '
paragraph1
paragraph2
' >>> html = cleaned_node_to_html(clean_node(fromstring(html))) >>> print(html)

paragraph1

paragraph2

```

functions.name: clear_html.clean.apply_text_exclusions
documentation:
Apply some additional exclusions that are needed to export the
body as text. Modify given node.

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.wrap_element_content_with_tag
documentation: Enclose the whole content of the given doc within a new and single child. The new created element is returned. Doc is updated in place. ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("hel") >>> el = wrap_element_content_with_tag(html, 'tag') >>> tostring(html).decode() 'hel' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("helh1t1") >>> el = wrap_element_content_with_tag(html.find('c'), 'tag') >>> tostring(html).decode() 'helh1t1' ```

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.wrap_element_with_tag
documentation: Enclose the given doc within a new tag maintaining the tree structure. The new created element is returned. Doc is updated in place. ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("hello") >>> tostring(wrap_element_with_tag(html, 'tag')).decode() 'hello' ``` ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("hello") >>> el = wrap_element_with_tag(html.find('b'), 'tag') >>> tostring(html).decode() 'hello' ```

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.prev_text
documentation: Return the text previous to the given node. Previous is parent text for first child nodes. ```pycon >>> html = fromstring("helzo") >>> prev_text(html) '' >>> prev_text(html.find(".//b")) 'h' >>> prev_text(html.find(".//c")) 'l' ```

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.iter_deep_first_post_order
documentation:
Iterate over a document in a deep first fashion returning
elements post-order https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tree_traversal#Post-order_(LRN)

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.wrap_children_slice
documentation:
Wraps a slice of children into the same tag. Return new created tag. ```pycon >>> orig_html = fromstring("wxyz") >>> html = copy.deepcopy(orig_html) >>> _ = wrap_children_slice(ChildrenSlice(html, 0, 3), 'div') >>> tostring(html).decode() 'w
xy
z' >>> html = copy.deepcopy(orig_html) >>> _ = wrap_children_slice(ChildrenSlice(html, 0, 1), 'div') >>> tostring(html).decode() 'w
xyz' >>> html = copy.deepcopy(orig_html) >>> _ = wrap_children_slice(ChildrenSlice(html, 2, 3), 'div') >>> tostring(html).decode() 'wxy
z' ```

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.ancestors
documentation:
Return the ancestors of a node ordered by distance.

```pycon
>>> tags = lambda x: list(map(lambda n: n.tag, x))
>>> nodes = [Element(tag) for tag in 'abcde']
>>> for i in range(1,len(nodes)):
...     nodes[i-1].insert(0, nodes[i])
```

```pycon
>>> tags(ancestors(nodes[4]))
['d', 'c', 'b', 'a']
>>> tags(ancestors(nodes[4], 0))
[]
>>> tags(ancestors(nodes[4], 2))
['d', 'c']
>>> tags(ancestors(nodes[4], 200))
['d', 'c', 'b', 'a']
>>> tags(ancestors(nodes[0], 200))
[]
>>> tags(ancestors(nodes[4], stop_at=nodes[1]))
['d', 'c', 'b']
```

functions.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.descendants
documentation: Return the descendant nodes of a given nodes until a particular level. All descendants are returned If no `max_level` is provided. ```pycon >>> tags = lambda x: list(map(lambda n: n.tag, x)) >>> html = fromstring("") >>> tags(descendants(html.find(".//b11"))) [] >>> tags(descendants(html.find(".//b"))) ['b1', 'b11', 'b2', 'b3'] >>> tags(descendants(html.find(".//b"))) == tags(descendants(html.find(".//b"), 2)) True >>> tags(descendants(html.find(".//b"), 0)) [] >>> tags(descendants(html.find(".//b"), 1)) ['b1', 'b2', 'b3'] ```

classes.name: clear_html.lxml_utils.ChildrenSlice
documentation:
Represents a slice of children withing a node. `node[start:end]`.
The slice containing root node is represented by a `None` node
